Rieschiecks.description: apparently is just a boring shrinker

Fisted/Casle\u2019s Tomb.witch_notes: Really hard to get into if you don’t want to get wet however I made a rope ladder to climb down which made it better. It’s a massive RCP with barely any graff in it except for super old Cave clan tags which was super interesting to see the history. Eventually goes up to a big grill room with a split to 2 small tunnels but nothing really worth doing after the grill. But still a really cool drain.

Drongo's Den.witch_notes: Not too much water but it is kind of low, which is annoying but about 100m in there’s a double split in a nice little room up the right pipe is a really cool grill room/overflow and the left pipe continues on to another grill room and double split. The right pipe does drop down extremely low halfway through it and you will need to crawl for about 100m until it opens back up again to a regular size. overall pretty cool.

B.U.R.P..witch_notes: Wasn't really much to the drain itself, but it was still pretty cool given how large it is and the area around it was really beautiful too. Still interesting to see despite not having many crazy features.

Witch\u2019s Tomb.witch_notes: low as balls but i think it has good potential

Eltham.witch_notes: Pretty wide and chill, definitely a nice walk but nothing to unique. There's a massive skylight/grille about halfway through that looks super cool. Only really worth it for a calm walk.

The Mission.description: The Mission🚫 👣 👁GPS -37.710766, 145.147436MELWAYS REF: 21 J-4LOCATION: On grounds of Judge Book Memorial Village. East bank of Diamond CkCLAN ZONE: North eastENTRANCE: 2m ⭕PROBLEMS: Finding & getting down to the entranceTRANSPORT: Train to ElthamVALUE: 🗑HIGHLIGHT: ☹CLEANLINESS: 🙂NEARBY: Eltham, Bridgette’s, Mitchelle’s & Tiffani’s⛔: Once in the drain it’s 🙂NOTES: Not very good, but maybe it’s better than we think? Prowler?

Flat.description: FlatGPS -37.799186, 145.115965MELWAYS REF. 47 B-4LOCATION: North end of Box Hill Cres over Belmore RdENTRANCE: 2 × 1.8m ⭕OTHER SHAPES: ☹CLAN ZONE: EastVALUE: ☹TRANSPORT: Ringwood train to Box Hill then busHIGHLIGHT: Joining roomCLEANLINESS: A bit of rubble at startPROBLEMS: Could be worse. ⭕ 5cm deepNEARBY: 10thNOTES: Goes up to 2.1m after the join. Drain just down from outlet is called Spectator.Song goes a flat tyre riding out to find Flat. Sloth & Woody each got a flat on the first expo.

Dougo's Tomb.description: Dougo’s Tomb🚫GPS -37.853695, 145.061038MELWAYS REF. 60 B-6LOCATION: 11A Denman Av (approx). Follow Back Creek with the flow from Sloth’s TombENTRANCE: 3.2 ⬜OTHER SHAPES: ⌒CLAN ZONE: EastVALUE: 🏆TRANSPORT: Alamein train to HartwellHIGHLIGHT: EntranceCLEANLINESS: Good except for the outlet⛔: Outlet kneePROBLEMS: ExitingNEARBY: Great Stairway, Echo Tunnel, Macka’s, Hexadus, Chameleon, Big Bastard & Sloth’sNOTES: The 1.6 side pipe has some nice bits for hardcore explorers. Do Dougo’s then Sloth’s and Great Stairway.Dougo’s Tomb is named after one of the founding Clan members who is also rumoured to be an iron-fisted wookie who has murdered some members.
Dougo's Tomb.witch_notes: Massive square tunnel and really easy on the feet, super fun walk and very chill. not many features to it except a little side pipe thats quite low that goes on for a while. but other than that, it’s just a really nice walk and overall a pretty chill drain.

NGCD.witch_notes: Cool entrance with two square tunnels leading in that both join up at the pillars and continue on eventually getting smaller which was pretty interesting. The pillars are the main feature with a bunch of grilles and another split and there’s cool stories about the drain given its significance to the cave clan but after the pillars there’s not really much else to it, just big tunnels that go on for a while. still pretty big and not a bad explore.

130 Steps.description:
130 Steps
🚫
GPS: -37.772286, 144.851544
MELWAYS REF: 27 C-9
LOCATION: Walk down the track located at the bend in Burke St. Turn right at river & check bank.
VALUE: 🙂
ENTRANCE: 1.6m ⚪
OTHER SHAPES: ☹️
CLAN ZONE: North west.
HIGHLIGHT: The stairways.
TRANSPORT: Bus from Albion stn to Burke St.
CLEANLINESS: Can get a bit webby.
WATER: shoes will get wet.
PROBLEMS: Low long stairway.
NEARBY: COAD, Funnel Web, BURP, FART & 3 Sisters.
NOTES: This drain is a good build up to Roxy's.
130 Steps takes its name from the fact that it has 130 steps in it... yes, it's true!

Holey Foot.witch_notes: Double barrel entrance with decently big pipes. not many features throughout the walk to the end just classic RCP, but the end has a big grill room which is the highlight. It was very cool and massive. overall nothing crazy but pretty cool.

Fish & Chipp.witch_notes: Has a really interesting entrance by a beautiful creek. The issue is that its very very low but despite that has some actually really interesting features. there’s some cool junctions manhole exits grills and a waterfall definitely on the difficult side.

Dweller's Tomb.description:
Dweller’s Tomb
🚫 🌊
GPS: -37.875146, 145.070992
MELWAYS REF: 69 B-1
LOCATION: Follow Gardiners Creek upstream from Solway St.
CLAN ZONE: East.
ENTRANCE: 2.8m ⚪
OTHER SHAPES: ⌂, ⬜ & ⬜
PROBLEMS: Length.
TRANSPORT: Glen Waverley train to East Malvern.
HIGHLIGHT: Old sections. Waiting room.
CLEANLINESS: A bit wet.
WATER: Entrance is knee deep unless you can do the splits.
VALUE: 🏆
NEARBY: Chameleon, iD’s Tomb, Dougo’s Tomb, Echo Tunnel and Mini 10th.
NOTES: The entrance can be a bit off-putting to newer explorers, but on a hot day, wow! Lots of changes included. You may do a bit of slipping & sliding. Don't ask about the fireman!
Dweller's Tomb is named after the group from the 80's, the Drain Dwellers. Map to the right >

The Maze.description:
The Maze
🍸
GPS: -37.823213, 145.023108
MELWAYS REF: 45 A-11
LOCATION: Next to flats on west side of Evansdale Rd between railway place and Austin Street over wooden fence.
ENTRANCE: 3.2m ⚪
OTHER SHAPES: ⌂, balloon, ⬜, ⌂ & ⬜
CLAN ZONE: East.
TRANSPORT: Train to Hawthorn
HIGHLIGHT: Length & changes. One of the top 3 drains in Australia.
WATER: 🙂
CLEANLINESS: Avoidable wet feet.
PROBLEMS: Doing it in one visit.
VALUE: 🏆
NEARBY: The Slide, Mega Slide, Ladders to Heaven, No Vest, Vampire's Cavern, CBRBD & Punctual.
NOTES: Do this drain on a nice hot day. There are a few exits & many junctions & features. A 10/10er. Bike can be used in sections. Follow white arrows where possible.
Maze is named due to the many splits and loops in the system.
The Maze.witch_notes: Huge drain system that spans on for ages has a triple split a little bit up with some really cool sections some red brick some bluestone and lots of different shape changes easily one of the best drain systems in Melbourne by far.

Great Stairway - Creek Entrance.witch_notes: Another really popular drain with a nice big tunnel comes up to a split the tunnel on the right goes to a cool grill room. The tunnel on the left leads on for a while that goes to the staircase which is the main feature between the tunnels as a crawlspace where you can get between the two which is really good to do the whole thing the staircase is massive with a big red brick RCP at the top of it cool shape changes and fun to explore.

Anzac.witch_notes: Coming in from the river outlet is a huge archway tunnel leading all the way down straight to the chamber. The chamber being the main feature is absolutely massive. The biggest strain in Melbourne I’d say really cool history and lots to look at in the chamber with some crazy architecture around at the end of the chamber. It leads down into two smaller pipes that are quite low and leads onto some very old tunnels that are also pretty fun to explore. The entrance I used to take it now blocked off so you had to go through the river entrance which means wet feet or a manhole entrance which I've never done before lol
Anzac.description: G.O.D. ⬜ 🚫 🧗GPS: 37.795754, 145.001252MELWAYS REF: 2D B-5CLAN ZONE: InnerLOCATION: On west bank of the Merri Ck. Between Eastern Fwy bridge & the Yarra.ENTRANCE: 3m ⭕OTHER SHAPES: Bowl (flat & curved top), ⭕ ⬜ (flat & angled floor)PROBLEMS: Some slippery sections.CLEANLINESS: You may get splashed climbing the waterfall.TRANSPORT: Clifton Hill train to Victoria ParkHIGHLIGHT: Variety in shape and texture.NEARBY: CBRD, Vampires Cavern, Mega Slide, Ladders To Heaven, Puncture, Short, Bangers Tomb, Northcote High, Flooded & Nam.NOTES: This is a definite summer drain. The waterfall is awkward during wetter periods plus the bluestone & angled floors can be hazardous. Big $ were spent on repairs in 1996/7 that gutted some sections.G.O.D. was named after the Grunt Owned Darkie.

Blade's Tomb.witch_notes: Honestly was pretty boring and didn't have much to it. it’s pretty low at the start and goes on for a while without much change but eventually it has some shape changes and a couple of interesting features like the split into 2 RCP’s. The canal outside the drain is really cool and is a really good walk but not my type of drain.
Blade's Tomb.description: Blade’s Tomb🚫 🚲 🍸GPS -37.900200, 145.008570MELWAYS REF: 67 H-7LOCATION: Follow canal against the flow Asling St through underpass. Check side tunnel in North Rd underpassENTRANCE: 2.8m ⬜OTHER SHAPES: ⭕CLAN ZONE: SouthVALUE: 🏆TRANSPORT: Sandringham train to Gardenvale⛔🙂PROBLEMS: 🙂HIGHLIGHT: OutletCLEANLINESS: 🙂NEARBY: Tommy’s Hole, Pauline, Underworld, Cobra’s & DungeonNOTES: When the tunnel splits into 2 RCPs take either as they eventually join up via a loop. See page 34 for map.Blade’s Tomb was named after one of the founders of The Drain Dwellers.

Clantomb.witch_notes: Clan tomb is a really good drain and a nice walk with very interesting features. There are steps, ramps, plenty of shape changes, and as you go further up, there is a very large rectangle corridor with lots of cool graph and a couple of grills eventually leading onto a split with two RCP‘s. My favorite thing about the drain is all of the shape changes and the layout is very interesting.

G.O.D..witch_notes: Extremely fucking long and straight, very interesting and unique shape changes. this drain is so goddamn straight I had a panick attack cause I walked for like an hour and a half in a dead straight line by myself and soon realized no manhole opens and the only exit was an extremely long walk back. not fun. Don't do by yourself. Honestly though this is a really nice drain I'm just skitz in drains like that.

Mini Tenth.witch_notes:
Super sick chamber as you walk in with cool decorations and a disco ball. The "Dog Room" was really interesting with photo frames of dogs on the wall and pictures plastered to the ceiling. further down it's a very wide drain with some small off-shoot tunnels but other than that slightly featureless.

Knightstalker's (9/10)
Amazing drain with super cool shape changes and waterfalls, awesome graffiti and really cool features. goes pretty far but keeps it interesting. We nearly drowned in here because it started raining which was really stupid of me but other than nearly dying it was incredible.

Slide.witch_notes: Had really nice red brick section at the start cool little chamber not too far in, but after that, they went over the red brick with concrete, which makes it look really ugly was hard to walk through and a little flooded at the time so we didn't make it up to the slide, but was still cool overall.  nothing too crazy.

Punctual.witch_notes: Really interesting entrance with a weird material black uphill tunnel, then a nice old red brick balloon shape that continues straight for a long time pretty boring after the first 60 meters but the start is super cool.

Predator's.witch_notes: Probably the best drain system I’ve ever been in. Insanely unique shapes, entrances, outlets and rooms. Has super cool hole in the roof that the sun pours in from running all the way down. Walls everywhere with 3 tunnels and all sorts of grilles and rooms. Easily the most interesting and best drain I’ve ever been to and seen in my life.

Glass Creek.witch_notes: Really nice walk easy on the feet and lots of space. The highlight is obviously the best feature with the room above the drain. Lots of cool history and old cave clan tags but after the highlight doesn’t offer a hell of a lot still pretty good overall. would do again.

Ringwood Bypass.witch_notes: The bypass is decently big about 5m tall and a triple barrel. The middle tunnel is flooded, but the other others aren’t. The left tunnel has a side pipe that goes to a grill room and also to an overflow in the middle of a lake, which is pretty cool but is a very low and decent crawl to get to it. to be honest not really the best

Gobbledox.witch_notes: This drain has such a beautiful entrance from the canal. Starts off pretty normal with the addition of a downward slope the whole way but gets massive about halfway through. Has a super tall ceiling and some interesting side pipes but not too many other features. It’s located in a really beautiful forest which was nice.

Spectator.witch_notes: The whole tunnel is low and pretty shit with no features other than a tiny grille room. not a lot to offer. but we took Maggie and she seemed to have a lot of fun so maybe not too bad.
